summary: Good soundboard recording of a varied 1989 show with strong Built To Last tunes and standout jams like Bertha, Bird Song and Crazy Fingers
review: The audience notes a solid sound‑board capture that’s free of major flaws and praises the band’s performance – especially the four‑song Built To Last suite, a well‑spaced Bird Song, a soaring Bob Weir on Picasso’s Dream and a fiery Crazy Fingers. While most songs are highlighted positively, Foolish receives a lukewarm nod compared to Victim.

ai_rating.rationale: Only one detailed review but it rates the show 4 stars and mentions no serious recording issues, suggesting a good performance and decent soundboard quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific audio complaints; assumed clear soundboard mix typical of the era.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A mixed set that blends newer, challenging material (the Built‑to‑Last suite) with classic jams and deep cuts, keeping the energy high throughout.

band_member_comments.Bob: Weir pushes his vocal range on Picasso's Dream, hitting high notes.
